statement: The V3 control-plane status path must remain cohort-scoped and read-budgeted (avoid whole-table scans) to support full-matrix pilots; otherwise, campaign monitoring will regress into read-limit failures even if Temporal execution is correct.
rationale: The previous V3 pass accumulated explicit read-limit and hot-path issues during cohort status monitoring, and those failure modes are orthogonal to the execution substrate. Temporal does not automatically solve Convex read-budget constraints.
keyAssumptions: The number of historical runs/windows/attempts will grow during pilot operation.
Cohort membership is known via experiment tags or pool bindings.
validationCriteria: getV3CampaignStatus (or its successor) can compute cohort state with bounded reads under worst-case cohort load.
The API surface can return a stable stuck summary and scientific validity judgment without scanning non-cohort rows.
critiquePoints: If the deployment remains small (few hundred runs), this might be premature.
If campaign operations move fully outside Convex, the read-budget problem could shift domains rather than disappear.
questions: Should cohort status be computed from an incremental materialized view (Convex table), or computed on demand with strict take() caps and indexes?
